user_query:
I need a one-shot daily AI news digest generated from curated mock RSS entries. Please do the following:

1) Read input/feeds.json and input/config.yaml. Use ONLY these files as your data source (do not fetch anything from the internet). Assume the JSON schema for input/feeds.json is an object with an array field "items", where each item has: source (string), title (string), link (string URL), summary (string), and published (ISO 8601 timestamp with timezone, e.g., 2026-04-17T08:00:00Z). Assume input/config.yaml provides these keys: now_utc (ISO timestamp), MAX_DAYS_OLD (int), MAX_ARTICLES_PER_FEED (int), MAX_TOTAL_ARTICLES (int).

2) Filter logic:
- Use now_utc from the config as the reference clock (do NOT use system time).
- Keep items whose published timestamp is within MAX_DAYS_OLD days before now_utc (inclusive) and not more than 1 day in the future relative to now_utc (strictly greater than +1 day should be excluded).
- Deduplicate across the entire set by link (case-insensitive) — keep the first occurrence only.

3) Capping logic:
- After filtering and de-duplication, limit each feed to at most MAX_ARTICLES_PER_FEED items (most recent first within that source), then ensure the overall set does not exceed MAX_TOTAL_ARTICLES (most recent first across the combined set is fine).

4) Output formatting:
- Create a Markdown digest in Simplified Chinese at output/digest.md. The body text for article titles and summaries should be in Simplified Chinese (you can translate the English content as needed). Group articles by their source with a section heading line using the pattern "### 📌 {source}".
- Start the file with a single header line that begins with the robot emoji (🤖) and includes the now_utc formatted exactly as YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM UTC somewhere on that line. On the next line, include the total number of included articles in the form "📰 {number} new items" (the digits can be Arabic numerals; the rest of the digest should be Chinese).
- For each article, include: (a) a bolded line for the title, (b) a blockquote line for the summary, and (c) a line containing a standard Markdown link that points to the original link URL. Leave a blank line between articles.

5) Machine-readable index:
- Also write output/index.json as an array of objects for the included articles. For each included article, compute an id as the lowercase hex MD5 of its link, and include at least these fields: id, source, link, published. Sort this JSON array by published descending.

Notes:
- Respect all caps and filtering rules exactly.
- Make sure your Chinese output is actually Chinese text (not just English with Chinese labels).
- Use only relative paths as provided above.
